mnemonics: PDA Pumps For CFLs - PushDown Automata (PDA) use a stack to Pump Context-Free Languages (CFL) for recognition.
keyPoints: A Pushdown Automaton (PDA) is an NFA equipped with a stack for memory.
PDAs are more powerful than Finite Automata and can recognize Context-Free Languages (CFLs).
The stack allows a PDA to handle non-regular constructs like matching parentheses.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that PDAs are inherently non-deterministic; a deterministic PDA is a smaller class.
Confusing the stack's LIFO principle with a general-purpose memory like a tape.
Incorrectly assuming all PDAs can be made deterministic, unlike finite automata.
